title: andropogon sorghum subsp. halepensis
intro: Andropogon sorghum subsp. halepensis, also known as Durra or Sudan grass, is a perennial grass species that belongs to the Poaceae family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe digestive issues and wounds. The medicinal properties of this plant can also provide relief from fever, headaches, and respiratory problems, making it a valuable herb for promoting overall health and well-being. Durra or Sudan grass can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions to harness its health benefits.
